metadata.name: Spectacle — Skewer the Critics cast for {R} after combat damage
metadata.description: Tests the Spectacle alternative cost mechanic (CR 702.137a). p1 controls Llanowar Elves (1/1) and a Mountain, and holds Skewer the Critics ({2R} Sorcery; Spectacle {R} — deals 3 damage to any target). Starting in precombat main, p1 advances through beginning_of_combat and attacks with Llanowar Elves in the declare_attackers step. p2 declares no blockers. In the combat_damage step, Llanowar Elves (power 1, unblocked) assigns 1 combat damage to p2 (p2 life: 40 to 39). Because p2 (an opponent) lost life this turn (life_lost_this_turn becomes 1 in the engine after combat damage, per CR 702.137b), the spectacle condition is satisfied. After advancing through end_of_combat to postcombat_main, p1 taps Mountain for {R} and casts Skewer the Critics for its spectacle cost {R} (instead of the normal {2R}), targeting p2. The spell resolves and deals 3 damage to p2 (p2 life: 39 to 36). Final life totals: p1 40, p2 36.
metadata.generation_notes:
Skewer the Critics (crates/engine/src/cards/defs/skewer_the_critics.rs): mana_cost {2R}, Sorcery, Spectacle {R}. Card definition has AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Spectacle), AbilityDefinition::Spectacle { cost: ManaCost { red: 1, ..Default::default() } }, and AbilityDefinition::Spell { effect: Effect::DealDamage { target: EffectTarget::DeclaredTarget { index: 0 }, amount: EffectAmount::Fixed(3) }, targets: vec![TargetRequirement::TargetAny], ... }.

Spectacle (CR 702.137a): An alternative cost. You may cast the spell for its spectacle cost instead of its mana cost if an opponent lost life this turn. CR 702.137b: An opponent of the caster must have had their life total reduced (lost life) this turn for the spectacle condition to be met. CR 118.9: Only one alternative cost may be applied when casting a spell.

Casting validation in casting.rs (line 1394-1404): the engine checks `state.players.iter().any(|(pid, ps)| *pid != player && !ps.has_lost && !ps.has_conceded && ps.life_lost_this_turn > 0)`. The `life_lost_this_turn` field on PlayerState (player.rs:131, #[serde(default)]) is NOT settable from the initial_state JSON (PlayerInitState in script_schema.rs lacks this field). Therefore the script must actually deal combat damage to p2 before casting with spectacle — the combat damage handler in combat.rs (line 1439) increments `player.life_lost_this_turn += final_dmg`.

Harness action type `cast_spell_spectacle` (replay_harness.rs:1033): sets `alt_cost: Some(AltCostKind::Spectacle)` on Command::CastSpell. No additional fields.

Phase transition pattern (confirmed from script_stack_132_dash_zurgo_bellstriker.json): Each step requires a `priority_round` (all players pass) to advance. Path from precombat_main: precombat_main -> beginning_of_combat -> declare_attackers -> declare_blockers -> combat_damage -> end_of_combat -> postcombat_main. Combat damage is applied via `assign_damage` player action. Each step boundary needs a priority_round.

Mana: Engine clears mana pools at phase transitions (CR 500.4). In postcombat_main, p1 taps Mountain for {R} (mana ability, CR 605.3) before casting Skewer the Critics.

Attacker choice — Llanowar Elves (1/1) NOT Monastery Swiftspear: Monastery Swiftspear has Prowess (CR 702.107), which triggers whenever its controller casts a noncreature spell. Casting Skewer the Critics (a Sorcery) in postcombat_main would put the Prowess trigger on the stack alongside Skewer the Critics (stack count 2), complicating the test. Llanowar Elves (1/1, {T}: Add {G}) has no triggered abilities that fire on casting noncreature spells, keeping the stack clean. Llanowar Elves has no summoning sickness on turn 4 (harness builder sets has_summoning_sickness=false for initial-state permanents).

Initial state: Turn 4. Phase: precombat_main. p1 has Llanowar Elves (1/1) and Mountain (untapped) on battlefield, Skewer the Critics in hand, empty mana pool (Mountain will be tapped for mana in postcombat main). p2 has no creatures.

Life math: p2 starts at 40. Combat damage from Llanowar Elves (power 1, unblocked) = 1. p2 life: 40 - 1 = 39. Skewer the Critics deals 3. p2 life: 39 - 3 = 36. p1 life stays at 40 throughout.

script.step_note: p1 is in precombat main. No spells cast. Both players pass priority — advance to beginning_of_combat.

actions.description: Initial: Llanowar Elves and Mountain untapped on p1 battlefield; Skewer the Critics in p1 hand; p2 at 40 life, no creatures; stack empty
actions.note: Llanowar Elves (1/1) and Mountain are on p1's battlefield, untapped, with no summoning sickness (turn 4, harness builder sets has_summoning_sickness=false). Skewer the Critics ({2R}, Spectacle {R}) is in p1's hand. p2 controls no creatures. Mana pool is empty — Mountain will be tapped in postcombat main for the spectacle cast.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in precombat_main with empty stack — advance to beginning_of_combat step.

script.step_note: Beginning of combat step. No triggered abilities fire. Both players pass priority to advance to declare_attackers.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in beginning_of_combat with empty stack — advance to declare_attackers step.

script.step_note: p1 declares Llanowar Elves as an attacker targeting p2. Llanowar Elves becomes tapped (CR 508.1f). Both players pass priority to advance to declare_blockers.

actions.note: CR 508.1: p1 declares Llanowar Elves as an attacking creature targeting p2. CR 508.1a: Llanowar Elves is untapped and free of summoning sickness — legal attacker. CR 508.1f: Llanowar Elves becomes tapped as a side effect of attacking.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Llanowar Elves is tapped, stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Llanowar Elves was tapped as part of the attack declaration. No triggered abilities fire (Llanowar Elves has no attack trigger).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: p2 declares no blockers (p2 controls no creatures). Llanowar Elves becomes an unblocked attacker (CR 509.1h). Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: p2 declares no blockers (no creatures to block with). CR 509.1h: Llanowar Elves has no creatures declared as blockers — it becomes an unblocked attacker.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage step.

script.step_note: p1 assigns all combat damage from Llanowar Elves (power 1, unblocked) to p2 (CR 510.1). p2 life: 40 to 39. The engine sets p2.life_lost_this_turn = 1, enabling spectacle in postcombat main (CR 702.137b).

actions.note: CR 510.1: p1 assigns all 1 point of combat damage from Llanowar Elves (power 1) to p2 — Llanowar Elves is unblocked (CR 510.1b). CR 120.3: p2 loses 1 life. The engine increments p2.life_lost_this_turn by 1 (combat.rs:1439), satisfying the spectacle condition (CR 702.137b).

actions.description: After combat damage: p2 at 39 life; Llanowar Elves still on p1 battlefield (tapped); stack empty; Mountain untapped
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Unblocked Llanowar Elves (power 1) dealt 1 combat damage to p2. p2 life: 40 to 39. life_lost_this_turn for p2 is now 1 — spectacle condition satisfied (CR 702.137b). Mountain remains untapped (will be used for mana in postcombat main).
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to end_of_combat step.

script.step_note: End of combat step. No triggered abilities fire. Both players pass priority to advance to postcombat_main.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in end_of_combat with empty stack — advance to postcombat_main phase.

script.step_note: p1 taps Mountain for {R} (mana ability, CR 605.3), then casts Skewer the Critics for its spectacle cost {R} (instead of normal {2R}) targeting p2. p2 lost life this turn (CR 702.137b). After resolution, p2 takes 3 damage: 39 to 36.

actions.note: CR 605.3: p1 activates Mountain's mana ability ({T}: Add {R}) — mana abilities don't use the stack and don't require priority. Mountain taps and p1 gains {R} in their mana pool.

actions.description: Before spectacle cast: Skewer the Critics in p1 hand, p2 at 39 life, Mountain tapped, stack empty
actions.note: Mountain is now tapped. p1 has {R} in mana pool from Mountain. CR 702.137b: p2 lost life this turn (1 combat damage from Llanowar Elves). Spectacle condition is satisfied — p1 may cast Skewer the Critics for {R}.

CR 702.137a: p1 casts Skewer the Critics for its spectacle cost {R} rather than its normal mana cost {2R}. CR 702.137b: p2 (an opponent) lost life this turn (1 combat damage from Llanowar Elves) — spectacle condition satisfied. CR 118.9: The spectacle cost is an alternative cost; only one alternative cost may be applied. Harness action 'cast_spell_spectacle' maps to AltCostKind::Spectacle on Command::CastSpell. Target: p2 (any target per Skewer's target requirement). Mana paid: {R} (spectacle cost) instead of {2R} (normal cost).

actions.description: After spectacle cast: Skewer the Critics on stack (count 1), p1 hand empty
actions.note: Skewer the Critics is on the stack with alt_cost=Spectacle. p1's hand is now empty. Stack count is 1 (Llanowar Elves has no Prowess or other triggered abilities that fire on casting noncreature spells).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack (Skewer the Critics) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 120.3: Skewer the Critics deals 3 damage to target player p2. The spectacle alternative cost affects only the mana paid — the spell's effect (3 damage) is unchanged regardless of how it was cast.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Skewer the Critics resolves. Effect: DealDamage Fixed(3) to DeclaredTarget{0} (p2). p2 life: 39 - 3 = 36. Spell moves to p1's graveyard.

actions.description: Final: p2 at 36 life (took 1 combat + 3 spell = 4 total), p1 at 40; Skewer the Critics in p1 graveyard; stack empty; Llanowar Elves still on p1 battlefield (tapped); Mountain tapped
actions.note: p2 life: 40 - 1 (combat damage from Llanowar Elves) - 3 (Skewer the Critics via spectacle) = 36. CR 702.137a: Spectacle allowed casting Skewer the Critics for {R} instead of {2R} because p2 (an opponent) lost life this turn (CR 702.137b). CR 118.9: Only one alternative cost was applied. Skewer the Critics is in p1's graveyard (resolved sorcery). Llanowar Elves and Mountain remain on p1's battlefield (both tapped).
